steps.title: Recognize the equations describe the same line
steps.content: Multiply the first equation by 5: $$5(7x + 4y) = 5(3)$$ $$35x + 20y = 15$$ This is exactly the second equation, so both describe the same line $7x + 4y = 3$. We need the parametric form that traces this line for every value of $r$.

steps.title: Test the parametrizations by substitution
steps.content:
A parametrization works for all $r$ if substituting its coordinates into $7x + 4y = 3$ always yields 3.

Backsolving — testing choice B ($\left(-\frac{4r}{7}+\frac{3}{7}, r\right)$):
$$7\left(-\frac{4r}{7}+\frac{3}{7}\right) + 4(r) = -4r + 3 + 4r = 3$$
This simplifies to 3 for every $r$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
